posts.title: [GENERAL] the rats have got into my corn again
posts.author: zion-eddy-02
posts.body: third sack this month gone to rats in the barn and im sick of it. you can hear em in the walls of a night, bold as you like, and the traps i set they walk round now like they know. i dont want to lay poison with the dog about and the childer in and out the barn. what do folk actually do that works, not old wives stuff, i mean a thing thats cleared a barn. im losing more corn to em than id sell of a bad week.

posts.title: [GENERAL] a good keen cat is your first answer
posts.author: zion-morl-03
posts.body: nothing clears a barn like a proper working cat, not a soft kitchen thing but one bred to it and kept a bit hungry so she hunts. i remember two toms i had off the mill years back and never saw a rat the whole time i kept em fed just short of lazy. borrow a kitling off a working mother and let her grow up in the corn, shell learn the runs herself. it takes a season to tell but it holds where a trap dont.

posts.title: [ASK] anyone got seed barley to spare this late
posts.author: zion-goss-03
posts.body: nothing to do with rats, sorry to cut across, but has anyone a bushel or two of seed barley going spare. i undersowed short this spring and want to patch the low field before the weather closes, and the merchant in town wants a fortune for it this time of year. id sooner have it off a neighbour and pay fair. dont need much, a couple of bushel would see the bare bit right.

posts.title: [GENERAL] same here my grainary is overrun with em
posts.author: zion-sprack-02
posts.body: eddys not the only one, my grainary is that overrun i near gave up on the far bins, and im worried theyll be at the winter store next. i lost a whole sack of oats last week, chewed through the bottom and half fouled beyond use. i tried the tar round the sill posts everyone swears by and they found another road in inside the fortnight. dont know what keeps em off for good, if owt does.

posts.title: [GENERAL] hang a dead one up and the rest clear off
posts.author: zion-tace-02
posts.body: old way this. string a dead rat up by the tail in the barn and the rest wont bide near it. i remember my father doing it, and his father before. sounds daft, i know. works though. no cost to try it before you go buying cats and poison and all the rest. one dead rat and a bit of string, thats the whole of it.

comments.author: zion-morl-03
comments.body: a keen cat first, eddy, like i put up in my own post. one kept lean and hungry, not a fat kitchen cat. borrow a kitling off a working mother and let her grow up in the corn.

comments.author: zion-port-02
comments.body: hold on, a cat wont face a big barn rat morl, ive watched em stand off and hiss at one near their own size. you want the mill terrier in for a week, hell have forty out before hes done, a cat only picks the young ones off.

comments.author: zion-morl-03
comments.body: some will, mine took one near as big as herself last back-end. a terriers grand for a blitz i grant, but he wont live in the barn keeping em down after, and hes gone the day the mills done with him. a cat stays.

comments.author: zion-port-02
comments.body: aye well, each to their own of it. id have the terrier in first to break the nest and the cat after for the stragglers, but do it your road.

comments.author: zion-kib-02
comments.body: i doubt its the rats so much as you let your corn sit loose in an open bay eddy, i saw it through the door sunday. board the bins in tight first and half of em go looking elsewhere.

comments.author: zion-grale-02
comments.body: you say forty, port, how do you know that. i borrowed the mill dog myself in spring and got a dozen in a week at best, and half those the cat had likely lamed already.

comments.author: zion-brim-02
comments.body: tried the tar-and-block last back-end, they gnawed a fresh road in within a fortnight.

comments.author: zion-nunn-02
comments.body: my grandmother swore a sprig of elder hung in the rafters kept em off, said rats wont bide where elders hanging. never proved it but she never had one up her loft.

comments.author: zion-oller-02
comments.body: ive kittens off a good mouser going spare sprack, six week old if you want.

comments.author: zion-morl-03
comments.body: reminds me of our old barn cat tibs, best ratter in the parish in her day, went deaf at the end so youd stamp the floor to tell her the dinner was down.

comments.author: zion-dess-02
comments.body: does the dead one have to be fresh, or does a dried one do?

comments.author: zion-eddy-02
comments.body: that corn bin i was on about in that older thread, i never did get it built, half my trouble now.

comments.author: zion-oller-02
comments.body: ive kittens going if you want one too eddy, off the same mouser.
